title: Young Baltic Energy Futures
summary: The training creates a cross-border space for learning and dialogue for young people to discuss the interconnection between energy supply, geopolitical stability, and climate action in the Baltic Sea region.
participants_from: Denmark, Estonia, Finland, Germany, Latvia, Lithuania, Norway, Poland, Sweden

title: STEAM in Youth Work: Creative projects for young people
summary: An introductory online workshop for youth workers to use STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Maths) in non‑formal education. Participants design simple, low‑threshold STEAM activities that foster creativity, teamwork and critical thinking.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Other countries in the world, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Virtual Partnership Building Activity (PBA) for Erasmus+ KA152 & KA153 Projects Vol. 4
summary: Virtual Partnership Building Activity for youth organizations to network, share project ideas, and build strong Erasmus+ KA152/KA153 partnerships. Join us on 1st and 2nd August to co-create impactful mobility projects!
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Sapounofouska Playful Learning Fest
summary: A 3-day outdoor experience for youth and educators combining STEAM, theatre, music-and-movement play, improv, group activities, nature, picnic, and party, ending each day with a hands-on 5E-based workshop.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Other countries in the world, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Supporting Volunteer's Mental Health through Mentoring
summary: Mentoring is one of the key elements to ensure meaningful European Solidarity Corps volunteering projects. People who work directly with volunteers benefit from understanding better, how the volunteers’ mental health can be promoted and supported.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

title: Belarus in Erasmus+ & European Solidarity Corps: Consultations for Belarusian Youth and Potential Partners
summary:
Are you a young person from Belarus interested in Erasmus+ or the ESC volunteering? 

Or an organization interested in working with Belarus but unsure how to navigate the current context?

Get a consultation with clear guidance and support.
participants_from: Eastern Partnership countries, Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

title: Inclusive Evolution: The Next Generation 2026
summary: We invite European representatives from NGOs, academia, and allied sectors to the Partnership Building Event 2026. Join like-minded professionals to explore synergistic opportunities and establish lasting partnerships. Registration deadline: 30.07.2026
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Other countries in the world, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Ask Better, Teach Better: Question‑driven AI & advocacy in youth work
summary: A hands-on online workshop for youth workers to design better questions – for their groups and for AI. Using empathy maps, fishbone and “How might we…?” prompts, participants turn real needs into meaningful learning and concrete actions.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Other countries in the world, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Robotics in Youth Work: From Playful Missions to Real Skills
summary: An online workshop using simple examples and mission‑based design, participants learn how to use robots to develop teamwork, problem‑solving and creativity with young people – even with limited equipment.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Other countries in the world, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: EU Paths and Perspectives
summary: EU Paths and Perspectives, part of the EU-ROOTS project, brings together youth workers from Ukraine and France to explore EU values, democracy, civic engagement, and youth work through learning, exchange, and cooperation.
participants_from: France, Ukraine

title: TOSCA Volunteering – Training and Support for Organisations in the European Solidarity Corps
summary: The training activity aims to support and build up the capacities of organisations that actively participate in volunteering actions of the European Solidarity Corps, in order to ensure quality and impact in the respective projects.
participants_from: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia, Moldova, Ukraine, Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Southern Mediterranean countries, Western Balkan countries

title: Learning by Bussing 2.0: from Denmark to Czechia
summary: Want to travel & learn? Join the Erasmus+ road trip Learning by Bussing (Sep 7–25) from Denmark to Prague! Run workshops, visit NGOs & meet new friends.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

title: Volunteering Teams as a Tool to support Inclusion
summary: Volunteering Teams as a Tool to support Inclusion partnership-building activity is for organisations looking for partners for volunteering teams, and for ideas on how to include inclusive elements in their projects.
participants_from: Armenia, Azerbaijan, Georgia, Moldova, Ukraine, Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Southern Mediterranean countries, Western Balkan countries

title: Understanding to Respond: Youth Work and Radicalisation
summary: This training course invites youth workers to explore how radicalisation develops, how beliefs and narratives shape young people’s attitudes, and how youth work can play a meaningful role in prevention and response.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

title: POWER: Project Factory for Empowerment of Young People In & After Imprisonment
summary: “POWER” is an international training course for professionals working with young people in and after imprisonment, bringing together institutions, probation services and NGOs to strengthen inclusion and reintegration.
participants_from: Eastern Partnership countries, Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries

title: KI in Erasmus+ Jugend und ESK – Digitalen Wandel aktiv gestalten (Deutschsprachig)
summary: Der Veranstaltungsort wird noch bekannt gegeben , Germany

title: PBA Youth X Culture
summary: Connect youth work & culture! This Leuven networking event helps cultural operators & youth groups build international partnerships funded by Erasmus+ and the European Solidarity Corps. Meet future partners & spark creative, inclusive projects.
participants_from: Erasmus+ Youth Programme countries, Partner Countries Neighbouring the EU

title: Participation, Engagement and Impact Lab for Multicultural Youth
summary: The training course is intended for organisations that work with multicultural youth or wish to strengthen diversity and inclusion within their activities and engage a broader range of young participants.
participants_from: Denmark, Estonia, Finland, Norway, Sweden

title: PBA Make the Move for Green Inclusion V
summary: PBA is organised to find good reliable partners for youth exchanges with a focus on inclusion and "green" and to understand the needs for a YE of good quality. For this time, youth workers and young people from same structures will come together.

title: Working with Queer Youth
summary: This training course brings together experienced LGBTQAI+ youth workers and organisations to exchange practices, strengthen community-based and participatory approaches, and explore connections between youth work and queer activism.
